title: OSMM Experience Object
description: A single deliverable experience's definition and decision — a winback email, a landing page variant, an ad set, a homepage hero, a triggered SMS. It models the DECISION (what the experience is, who it's for, what it's assembled from, its variants/personalization, its QA/approval status, and its deployment), NOT the rendered HTML/image, which lives in the ESP/CMS/DAM and is referenced via delivery_reference. This object absorbs the former Experience Specification (6.1 — the spec/intent), Personalization Configuration (6.4 — variant targeting), Experience Validation (6.6 — QA/readiness as a status), and Campaign Deployment's per-experience go-live (6.7), plus Experience Delivery itself (6.5). It is assembled from Experience Component objects (6.2), referenced by id. Canonical schema for object_type 'experience'. The osmm-experience-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

experience_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'EXP-' + lowercase hyphen slug. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: The experience's go-to-market name (e.g. 'Baconator App Winback Email', 'watsonx Trial Landing Page').

experience_type.description: Governed experience-type vocabulary. Pick the single closest value.

channel.description: The channel this experience is delivered through (e.g. 'Email', 'Web', 'Paid social'). Optional.

objective.description: What this experience is for — the outcome or job it does within the journey/campaign.

specification.description: The experience's spec/intent — scope, dependencies, requirements, and success criteria (the former Experience Specification, 6.1). Optional.

scope.description: What this experience does and does not cover.

dependencies.description: What this experience depends on (assets, data, upstream decisions).

requirements.description: Functional/content requirements the experience must meet.

success_criteria.description: What 'done/working' looks like for this experience.

linked_components.description: Ids of the Experience Components (6.2) this experience is assembled from. 'EXC-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

structure.description: The assembled layout/sections in order (e.g. headline -> hero -> body -> CTA). Optional.

variants.description: Channel-specific or A/B variations of this experience (folded from Experience Delivery, 6.5). Optional.

personalization_rules.description: Audience/condition-driven treatments (the former Personalization Configuration, 6.4). Optional.

condition.description: The condition that selects this treatment.

treatment.description: The personalized treatment applied when the condition is met.

audience.description: Optional Audience id the rule targets. 'AUD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok.

validation.description: QA / compliance / approval readiness as a status (the former Experience Validation, 6.6). Optional.

qa_notes.description: QA findings, checks run, or issues to resolve.

approved_by.description: Who approved the experience for release.

deployment.description: The per-experience go-live (the former Campaign Deployment per-experience activation, 6.7). Optional.

window.description: The activation window (when it goes/went live).

activation_notes.description: Go-live notes — targeting cutovers, ramp, dependencies.

delivery_reference.description: URL/id of the rendered asset in the production tool (ESP/CMS/DAM/ad platform). The rendered experience is referenced here, not stored in this object. Optional.

linked_campaign_strategy.description: Id of the Campaign Strategy this experience serves. 'CMS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_journey.description: Id of the Journey this experience serves. 'JNY-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_audiences.description: Ids of the Audiences this experience is for. 'AUD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_offer.description: Id of the Offer this experience activates. 'OFR-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_creative_strategy.description: Id of the Creative Strategy this experience expresses. 'CRS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context. 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date. Optional.
